title: hydrangea stellata
intro: Hydrangea stellata, also known as the snowball hydrangea, is a type of flowering shrub native to Japan, characterized by its white, round clusters of small flowers resembling snowballs. The plant's roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and protect against cell damage. Drinking a tea made from the roots of the hydrangea stellata is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using them for medicinal purposes.
